2023 Tucson PHEV
Jack Points & Install Guide

Misha's Adventure Build — Mobile Reference

Lift Point Overview

Pinch weld jack stands (1-4)
Front subframe (floor jack)
Rear crossmember (floor jack)
No-Jack Zone

The PHEV traction battery (360V) sits under the center of the car between the front and rear seats. Never place a jack or stand under the battery pan.

Vehicle Weight
~4,400 lbs
Per-Corner Load
~1,100 lbs
Lug Torque
80 ft-lbs
Lug Socket
21mm
Lug Pattern
5-star
Re-Torque
50-100 mi

Pinch Weld Jack Points

There are 4 pinch weld lift points — two per side, located along the rocker panels. Each is marked by two V-shaped notches cut into the weld seam, about 2 inches apart.

How to Find Them

Look along the rocker panel (the bottom edge of the car body between the front and rear wheels). You'll see:

  • The body cladding is cut away — there's a visible gap in the plastic trim
  • Two small V-shaped notches stamped into the metal pinch weld, about 2" apart
  • You lift between the two V-notches
Jack Pad Placement

Use the magnetic pinch weld jack pad (HF SKU 70888). It has a slot that cradles the pinch weld seam without crushing it. The magnet holds it in place while you position the jack.

Place the pad between the V-notches, position the jack saddle under the pad, and lift slowly.

Subframe & Crossmember Points

These are for lifting the full front or full rear of the car with a floor jack. You then place jack stands on the pinch welds.

Front Subframe (Lifting Both Front Wheels)

Front Lift Procedure
  1. Chock both rear wheels
  2. Place floor jack under the center of the front subframe crossmember — it's a thick steel beam running left-to-right under the engine area
  3. Lift until both front wheels clear the ground
  4. Place jack stands on both front pinch weld points
  5. Lower jack onto stands, verify stability

Rear Crossmember (Lifting Both Rear Wheels)

Rear Lift Procedure
  1. Chock both front wheels
  2. Place floor jack under the center of the rear crossmember — behind the rear differential, between the rear pinch weld points
  3. Lift until both rear wheels clear the ground
  4. Place jack stands on both rear pinch weld points
  5. Lower jack onto stands, verify stability
PHEV Warning

The battery pan is forward of the rear crossmember. When sliding the floor jack under from the rear, aim for the crossmember — do not let the jack contact the battery pan.

Wheel Swap Procedure

One corner at a time. OEM 19" off, Enkei TFR 17" on.

Before You Start

  • Park on flat, hard surface (concrete or asphalt, not gravel)
  • Car in P, parking brake ON
  • Engine/Ready mode OFF
  • Gather tools, have all 4 new wheels nearby

Per-Corner Steps

1
Chock the diagonally opposite wheel. (FR up → chock RL)
2
Loosen all 5 lug nuts ½ turn with breaker bar + 21mm socket while wheel is on the ground. Do not remove.
3
Position jack under pinch weld point with magnetic jack pad. Lift until tire clears ground by ~1 inch.
4
Place jack stand under the same pinch weld (slightly offset from jack). Lower jack until car rests on stand. Leave jack in contact as backup.
5
Remove all 5 lug nuts. Pull off the OEM wheel. Set aside.
6
Clean hub face with brass wire brush. Spray with brake cleaner. Wipe dry. Remove all corrosion and debris.
7
Anti-seize — thin layer on the hub spigot (center post) only. NOT on lugs or ring
8
Press centering ring onto hub spigot. Use the plastic Tire Rack rings now; swap aluminum StanceMagic rings when they arrive Monday.
9
Mount new wheel. Align holes, slide onto hub studs. Hand-thread all 5 lug nuts finger-tight.
10
Snug lugs in star pattern with the torque wrench. Just snug — do NOT final torque yet (wheel needs to be on the ground).
11
Jack up slightly off the stand. Remove stand. Lower car to ground slowly.
12
Final torque all 5 lugs to 80 ft-lbs in star pattern with wheel on the ground.
13
Move chock to this wheel. Repeat steps 1-12 on next corner.
After All 4 Wheels
  • Drive 50-100 miles of normal driving
  • Re-torque all 20 lugs to 80 ft-lbs
  • Run TPMS relearn procedure

Hitch Install Notes

Curt 13485 Class III hitch — no-drill install on the Tucson NX4 frame.

Lift Setup for Hitch

Recommended Configuration

Lift the full rear using the rear crossmember with the floor jack. Place both jack stands on the rear pinch welds. This gives you clearance underneath to work on the hitch mounting points.

You may want to remove both rear wheels for better access.

Additional Tools Needed

  • 19mm socket — for hitch mounting bolts (add to HF run)
  • Trim panel removal tools — for rear bumper fascia (in HF cart)
  • Fish wire or coat hanger — to route bolts behind frame
  • Flathead screwdriver — for push-pin retainers

Bumper Fascia

Partial Removal Required

The rear bumper fascia (plastic cover) must be partially removed to access the hitch mounting points. It's held by push-pin retainers and clips. Use the trim tools to pop them without breaking them. Mark each clip location so you remember where they go back.

Hitch Torque Spec

Refer to the Curt 13485 instruction sheet for specific bolt torque values. Typical Curt Class III hitch bolts torque to 50-75 ft-lbs but always follow the included instructions.

Official Manual References

Extracted from the 2023 Tucson NX4 Owner's Manual and Hyundai Service Manual. Tap any image to zoom.

Service Manual — Lift Block Positions

Front & Rear Lift Block Diagram

Official two-post lift support points — same pinch weld locations you'll use with your floor jack and stands.

Hyundai Service Manual — Lift and Support Points

Owner's Manual — Jack Point Locations

All 4 Jack Positions on Vehicle

White dots mark each pinch weld jack point. Point A = wheel chock position.

Owner's Manual p.8-15 — Jack Point Overview
Pinch Weld Locations & Jack Close-Up

Shows the front and rear pinch weld points on the rocker panel with the scissor jack positioned correctly between the V-notches.

Owner's Manual p.8-16 — Pinch Weld Jack Positions
Jack Under Vehicle

Scissor jack seated on the pinch weld, lifting the vehicle. With your floor jack, use the magnetic pinch weld pad in the same position.

Owner's Manual p.8-16 — Jack Raising Vehicle

Owner's Manual — Lug Nut Patterns

Loosening Pattern (counterclockwise)

Loosen lugs in this numbered order (1→2→3→4→5) one turn each before lifting.

Owner's Manual p.8-16 — Lug Loosening Order
Tightening Star Pattern

Tighten lugs in star pattern to 79-94 ft-lbs (80 ft-lbs is the safe target).

Owner's Manual p.8-17 — Lug Tightening Order

Jack Safety Label

OEM Jack Label Reference

Safety rules from Hyundai: always use designated jacking positions, always apply parking brake, never go under a jack-supported vehicle (use stands).

Owner's Manual p.8-14 — Jack Label

Tire Pressure & Bauer Inflator/Deflator

Your Setup

Tires: Falken Wildpeak A/T4W 245/65R17 XL — max 50 PSI

Door placard: 36 PSI (OEM spec)

Tool: Bauer 20V/12V Dual Power Inflator/Deflator (SKU 64533)

Power: 12V DC adapter plugged into cigarette lighter

Target Pressures by Terrain

Pavement 36 PSI Daily driving, highway
Gravel / Fire Road 30–32 PSI More comfort & grip
Dirt / Mud Trail 25–28 PSI Better traction
Sand / Deep Soft 18–22 PSI Float — minimum safe
Do Not Go Below 18 PSI

The Tucson PHEV weighs ~4,500 lbs. Without beadlock wheels, going below 18 PSI risks popping the bead off the Enkei TFR rims. 20 PSI is a safe practical floor.

Bauer Controls — Manual Reference (p.9)

Bauer 64533 Owner's Manual — Setup & Functions

How to Inflate & Set Target PSI — Manual Reference (p.11)

Bauer 64533 Owner's Manual — Inflating Car Tires

Inflate Procedure (Quick Reference)

  1. Plug 12V DC adapter into cigarette lighter socket (car in ACC or ON)
  2. Attach high-pressure hose to tire valve — lift lever up, push onto stem, lock lever down
  3. Press Power button — displays current tire pressure
  4. Press Power to cycle units: PSI → BAR → kPa
  5. Use PSI + / PSI − to set your target pressure (e.g. 36 PSI)
  6. Flip Pump Selection Switch to High Pressure (I) — inflating starts
  7. Pump auto-shuts off when target PSI is reached
  8. Flip switch to Off (O), unlock lever, remove from valve, replace cap
Cool-Down Rule

Let the Bauer cool for 10 minutes after every 10 minutes of continuous use. If doing all 4 tires from low pressure, take a break between the 2nd and 3rd tire.

How to Deflate — Manual Reference (p.12)

Bauer 64533 Owner's Manual — Deflating Procedure

Deflate Procedure (Quick Reference)

Important: Deflate Uses the LOW PRESSURE Hose & Air Intake Port
  1. Remove the Low Pressure Hose from its storage area
  2. Insert one end of the hose fully into the Air Intake port (not the output!)
  3. Connect the other end to the tire valve stem
  4. Press Power button
  5. Flip Pump Selection Switch to Low Pressure (II) — deflating starts
  6. Watch the gauge and flip switch to Off (O) when you reach your target PSI
  7. Remove hose, hold Power to turn off, replace valve cap
Deflate Does NOT Auto-Stop

Unlike inflate mode, the deflate function does not have a programmable auto-shutoff. You must watch the gauge and manually stop it at your target pressure. Don't walk away!

Trail Air-Down Workflow

  1. Stop on level ground before the trail
  2. Check current pressure with the Bauer gauge (press Power, read display)
  3. Deflate all 4 tires to target (e.g. 25 PSI for dirt)
  4. Drive the trail — go slowly for first few minutes to let tires settle
  5. Before pavement: re-inflate all 4 to 36 PSI using the 12V adapter
  6. Tip: bring a 12V extension cord (12 ft) so you can reach all 4 tires without moving the plug
12V Adapter Fuse

The Bauer's 12V adapter has a 10 amp fuse inside the plug tip. If it stops working, unscrew the end of the adapter and replace with a 10A fuse. Keep a spare in your glovebox.

Manual Source

Bauer 64533 Owner's Manual — Download Full PDF

Lower Tail Light Assembly Replacement

Replacing the lower bumper tail light assemblies (backup, turn signal) on the 2023 Tucson PHEV — OEM part numbers 92405-N9000 (driver/left) and 92406-N9000 (passenger/right).

Two Approaches

Service manual method: Remove entire rear bumper, then unbolt assemblies. Complete access but way more work than needed for this job.

Shortcut method (recommended): Access from underneath using ramps. Remove the rear side under cover (clips + screws), then unbolt the lamp assemblies from below. No bumper removal required.

What You're Replacing

The lower bumper lamp assemblies sit in the rear bumper fascia below the main tail lights. Each assembly houses:

  • Backup/reverse lamp — W16W bulb
  • Rear turn signal — PY21W amber bulb

These are the full housing assemblies — lens, reflector, and sockets are all integrated. Your new OEM units come complete; just transfer the existing bulbs if the new ones don't include them.

Tools & Supplies

Required
  • Drive-up ramps + wheel chocks (rear wheels up for access)
  • Phillips #2 screwdriver (cross-tip) — for lamp mounting screws
  • Trim removal tool (plastic pry tool) — for under-cover clips
  • 10mm socket + ratchet — for wheel house trim screws
  • Long-nose pliers (4" nose, 8" handle) — for tight-space clips
  • Work light / headlamp — it's dark under there
  • Safety glasses
  • Gloves — Hyundai service manual requires them
Helpful extras
  • Magnetic parts tray — don't lose screws under the car
  • Painter's tape — wrap screwdriver tips to avoid scratching bumper paint
  • Creeper or old blanket — you'll be on your back
  • Phone/camera — photograph connector orientation and screw locations before removing

Step-by-Step Procedure

Safety First

Disconnect the negative (−) battery terminal before starting. This prevents shorts when disconnecting lamp connectors. The 12V battery is under the hood, driver side.

Set parking brake, car in P, engine OFF. Chock front wheels before driving rear wheels onto ramps.

1
Ramp up the rear end
Drive rear wheels onto ramps. Set parking brake. Place wheel chocks in front of the front tires. Turn off engine. This gives you the clearance to work underneath the rear bumper.
2
Disconnect 12V battery negative terminal
Pop the hood, use a 10mm wrench to loosen the negative (−) cable clamp. Tuck the cable aside so it can't contact the terminal. Wait ~30 seconds before proceeding.
3
Remove the rear side under cover
Slide under the rear of the car. Locate the plastic under cover panel on the side you're working on. It's held by mounting clips (push-pin style) and possibly screws into the wheel house trim.
  • Use your trim tool to pop the center pin out of each push-clip, then pull the clip collar free
  • Loosen any Phillips or 10mm screws along the wheel house edge
  • Pry the under cover trim inward toward the vehicle and remove it

Passenger side extra step: There is a black plastic trim piece with 2 thumb screws (hand-removable) and 2 push-clips. Remove this piece first to access the lamp assembly behind it.

4
Photograph everything
Before disconnecting anything, take photos of: the electrical connector orientation, the mounting screw locations, and how the assembly sits in the bumper. This makes reinstall much easier.
5
Disconnect the electrical connector
Locate the wiring connector on the back of the lamp assembly. This is the same yellow CPA (Connector Position Assurance) tab style connector used throughout the rear.
  • Slide the yellow CPA tab backward (away from the connector body) — it must move ~5mm before the main latch is free to operate
  • Press the main release tab/lock pin inward
  • Pull the connector straight out — don't yank sideways
6
Remove the lamp assembly mounting screws
With the under cover out of the way, you'll see the Phillips mounting screws holding the lamp assembly to the bumper fascia. There are typically 2-3 screws per assembly.
  • Use a #2 Phillips screwdriver — a stubby or right-angle driver helps in tight spots
  • If a screw is hard to reach straight-on, try a flexible extension or universal joint on a 1/4" ratchet with a Phillips bit
  • Keep the magnetic tray close — dropped screws vanish under cars
7
Remove the old assembly
With screws out and connector disconnected, the assembly should pull free from the bumper. It may have alignment tabs or locating pins that fit into slots in the bumper — note their position. Gently wiggle and pull rearward/downward to release.
8
Transfer bulbs if needed
If the new OEM assemblies don't include bulbs, transfer them from the old housings:
  • Rotate the bulb socket counterclockwise until tabs align with slots, then pull socket out
  • Remove bulb from socket: press in and rotate counterclockwise, then pull straight out
  • Install into the new assembly in reverse order
9
Install the new assembly
Align the locating pins/tabs with the bumper slots. Press the assembly into position. Reinstall the mounting screws — snug them down but don't overtighten (plastic bumper fascia can crack). Reconnect the electrical connector until the CPA tab clicks back into its locked position.
10
Reinstall under covers and trim
Reverse of step 3. Pop the push-clips back in, reinstall any screws, and on the passenger side reinstall the black trim piece with its 2 thumb screws and 2 clips.
11
Reconnect battery and test
Reconnect the negative battery terminal. Start the car and verify:
  • Turn signals blink (activate hazards to test both at once)
  • Reverse lights illuminate (shift to R with foot on brake)
  • No warning lights on dash
12
Drive off ramps
Remove chocks from front wheels. Start car, release parking brake, and slowly reverse off the ramps. Done!

Tips for Hard-to-Reach Screws

  • Stubby Phillips screwdriver — when there's no room for a full-length driver above the screw
  • 1/4" ratchet + Phillips bit + universal joint — lets you attack screws at an angle
  • Flexible bit extension — bends around obstructions; slow and steady wins
  • Magnetic bit holder — keeps the screw on the bit when you can't see or reach with your other hand
  • Headlamp (not handheld flashlight) — both hands free is critical underneath the car
  • Wrap screwdriver shafts and pry tools with painter's tape to protect the bumper paint per the service manual recommendation

Driver vs. Passenger Side Differences

Driver side (92405-N9000): Generally easier access. The under cover comes off and you have a relatively clear path to the mounting screws and connector.

Passenger side (92406-N9000): Harder. There's an extra black plastic trim panel that covers the assembly area, held by 2 hand-removable thumb screws and 2 push-clips. Remove this first, then proceed the same as the driver side. Budget extra time for this side.

Do One Side at a Time

Finish the driver side completely first — it's easier and teaches you the process. Then tackle the passenger side knowing what to expect. Having the first old assembly as a reference while installing the second side helps.

Time Estimate

Driver side: ~20-30 minutes
Passenger side: ~30-45 minutes (extra trim removal)
Total with battery disconnect/reconnect and testing: ~1-1.5 hours for both sides
